title: Yeast growth assays and quantification
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1gu8u","text":"Growth of yeast cells on solid media to test for functionality of endogenous or heterologous ammonium transporters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transform and select yeast on solid YNB (minimal yeast medium without nitrogen) medium supplemented with 3% glucose and 1 mM arginine. For selection, grow cells in liquid YNB medium supplemented with 3% glucose and 1 mM arginine for one day, dilute 10, 100, 1000 and 10000 in water and drop 5 µL of cells on solid YNB medium containing 50 mM Tris/MES buffered at pH 5.2, 3% glucose and 0.2, 0.4 or 2 mM ammonium chloride or, as a control, 1 mM arginine.
Incubate cells 3-6 days at 28°C and document results by scanning the plates at 600dpi in grey scale.

Quantify yeast growth using ImageQuant TL software (v2003, Amersham). Subtract the average pixel-intensity of each 1000 dilution spots from the agar-intensity-background and normalize with intensity of controls.
A cytosolic trans-activation domain essential for ammonium uptake by Loqué, D. et al. published in Nature on 20 Dec, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature05579
creator: Protocols Importer
